120558.explanation: We want a council that is a good reflection of society and fits with an equal male-female ratio. But quality and administrative suitability must be leading in the composition, not a mandatory percentage.

120562.explanation: Farmers must have space to undertake and diversify, for example with a shop or small-scale recreation. But this must fit the environment and within clear rules for safety, traffic, and livability.

120563.explanation: If residents want to generate sustainable energy together, that deserves support. Municipal Interests wants to expand municipal sustainability subsidies to encourage sustainability.

120566.explanation: In principle, housing belongs to entrepreneurship. Preference is for housing on or near the business, but always with clear conditions and good enforcement. Housing on-site is not desirable or spatially possible everywhere; customization must remain possible.

120570.explanation: Municipal Interests would certainly support an initiative by residents and/or entrepreneurs, provided it complies with laws and regulations. However, this must not come at the expense of community funds. The municipality should facilitate this if possible but not pay for it.

120571.explanation: Free curbside bulky waste collection sounds attractive but costs a lot of money and organization. We want to keep municipal costs manageable. Municipal Interests chooses a recycling center nearby with a circular craft center, where discarded goods and materials are given a new life.

120573.explanation: Gemeentebelang wants everyone to participate; for that, good accessibility for people with disabilities is essential. Therefore, we advocate for accessibility of public spaces and buildings. We can advise and support entrepreneurs.

120574.explanation: Housing construction is urgently needed and farmers can help with this, but housing in rural areas is only possible under clear conditions. Policy already exists in Nunspeet for this, such as change of use/red-for-red schemes, with a focus on quality improvement. Building is allowed if it fits into the landscape.

120576.explanation: Our local broadcaster is very important for our local news provision. The new media law threatens to dismantle that. Gemeentebelang is committed to preserving our broadcaster and wants to creatively explore additional funding where needed, for example by the municipality purchasing more broadcasting time.

120578.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate, even if your parents have little money. Music lessons are good for development and talent. That is why we want to keep music lessons accessible through targeted support for families who need it. We want to maintain existing arrangements for this.

120579.explanation: Sports clubs are indispensable. Nunspeet already supports sports through subsidies and facilities. Gemeentebelang has also worked hard to keep sports field rental prices low. If clubs face difficulties, targeted support must remain possible.

120582.explanation: We want sufficient visible enforcement. Due to the broader range of tasks and limited capacity, we must first better prioritize what we ask from enforcement officers (BOAs). If that proves insufficient, increasing the number of BOAs is an option.

120583.explanation: Camera surveillance can contribute to (social) safety, but it is not an end in itself. We want targeted deployment where possible and desirable, with respect for privacy and combined with visible enforcement.

120587.explanation: Holiday homes are intended for recreation, and that must remain so. Structural housing shortages should be solved by appropriate housing for our own residents. For urgent cases, there is the pause home scheme. Gemeentebelang supports more pause homes.

120588.explanation: The housing shortage requires speed and space. We want to build according to our residents’ needs; that is not possible if we only build within village boundaries. Large housing locations like 't Hul Noord are necessary to address the housing shortage.

120590.explanation: Residents with a low income often already struggle to make ends meet. Waiving fees is necessary to prevent people with low incomes from getting into further trouble, so that everyone can continue to participate.

120591.explanation: In Nunspeet, we already apply social and generous policies for minimum arrangements, such as setting the income limit for individual income supplements at 120% of the social assistance standard.

120594.explanation: Traffic safety is a priority, especially for children. Therefore, Gemeentebelang supports 30 km/h zones in residential areas and near schools and crossings, as part of safe school routes, but not automatically throughout the entire built-up area.

120595.explanation: Care must match a person’s situation, background, and wishes. We value freedom of choice, but it must not lead to too many (care) providers and too much overlap between welfare organizations. The municipality and care providers must deliver customized solutions where possible.

120597.explanation: Housing costs must remain affordable. Fortunately, the municipality of Nunspeet already has relatively low property tax (OZB), and we want to keep it that way. Gemeentebelang wants to first critically examine municipal expenses and work more efficiently. At the same time, costs are rising, so lowering the OZB is not realistic.

120599.explanation: Tourism is very important for our local economy. Raising the tourist tax makes Nunspeet more expensive for visitors and is bad for businesses. An increase is sometimes justifiable, for example if it is accompanied by better facilities for tourists.

120602.explanation: Municipalities have a legal duty to house status holders but are already unable to fully meet this. Gemeentebelang wants to make as many homes as possible available to our own residents; it is up to The Hague to come up with solutions.

120604.explanation: Gemeentebelang wants to handle our landscape carefully; the municipality of Nunspeet already prioritizes 'solar panels on roofs first.' Solar fields on agricultural land are only possible under strict conditions. No generic ban, but clear boundaries.

120608.explanation: Space to build is scarce. Therefore, we want to build higher in more places, provided this fits within the environment. No urban gimmicks like excessively tall apartment buildings, but more efficient use of scarce space.

120611.explanation: Gemeentebelang expects that due to increasing traffic and growth, tunneling will be desirable over time and has requested a study, which will also be carried out. Allocating funds now is premature.

120558.explanation: Suitability is the most important factor, and that must be the principle for assessment.

120562.explanation: Not “always.” Space for ancillary activities where the zoning plan, nature, traffic, and surroundings allow.

120563.explanation: Targeted support for local energy cooperatives (start/design). No open-ended subsidies; results and support base count.

120566.explanation: Yes, employers provide appropriate housing according to standards; this protects village centers and prevents scarcity.

120570.explanation: Not a core municipal task: focus on safety, enforcement, and cost control. The municipality can facilitate, permit, and if added value is demonstrated, partly subsidize.

120571.explanation: Free collection increases costs and misuse, which raises residents' charges (waste disposal fees), meaning it is ultimately paid by residents themselves.

120573.explanation: Small, targeted support accelerates accessibility; conditions and personal contributions safeguard this.

120574.explanation: Scattered residential development in rural areas damages the landscape.

120576.explanation: Basic funding is sufficient. Extra money only for demonstrable added value and performance agreements.

120578.explanation: Talent development is good but no generic subsidy. Focus on low-income groups via existing funds.

120579.explanation: We want to optimize existing sports policy, distributing subsidies more fairly.

120582.explanation: First optimize the effectiveness and deployment of enforcement officers (BOAs). Plan and enforce smarter instead of generic expansion.

120583.explanation: A previous pilot showed that local camera surveillance has no added value.

120587.explanation: Permanent residence in parks is not allowed; exceptions only temporarily and carefully, not as a loophole.

120588.explanation: Restrained development outside contours. First densify and transform within existing areas.

120590.explanation: Maintain fee waivers for low incomes; debts and poverty require tailored solutions, not a generic stop.

120591.explanation: Focus help on those who really need it; expand where demonstrably effective, not broadly without justification.

120594.explanation: 30 km/h where appropriate. Traffic safety through tailored measures, design, and enforcement; no generic speed reduction.

120595.explanation: Freedom of choice in care is important; respect for religion, culture, and convictions within quality requirements.

120597.explanation: The municipality must focus on core tasks so that property taxes (OZB) can be lowered, provided this is structurally covered and core tasks are not compromised.

120599.explanation: Tourist tax can be used for developments that also benefit tourists. No automatic additional increase.

120602.explanation: Implementing the task assignment according to the law, but no extra local priority above other groups.

120604.explanation: No solar fields on agricultural land. Focus on roofs, facades, and parking areas with canopies.

120608.explanation: High-rise flats do not fit with the scale of Nunspeet, although we cannot avoid building somewhat higher where possible (4 or in exceptions 5 stories).

120611.explanation: The Eperweg tunnel is crucial for safety and traffic flow; co-financing and phasing are necessary.

120558.explanation: It is important that the executive board has the qualities to implement the council or coalition agreement. This requires knowledge and skills that both men and women can have. A board composed entirely of men is not our preference.

120562.explanation: We are careful with agricultural land and at the same time take our farmers very seriously in the plans they make for the sustainability of their farms. Always allowing it would mean ignoring the residents, nature, and the landscape. So, there are conditions.

120563.explanation: The energy transition is an opportunity to make the municipality of Nunspeet stronger, greener, and more independent. That is why we choose courage, cooperation, and tangible change. Citizen initiatives must be financially supported and administrative burdens kept as low as possible.

120566.explanation: We stand for dignified housing of labor migrants. Residential locations are required to meet the SNF quality mark, including the norm of one resident per bedroom, to promote the safety and privacy of labor migrants. Housing outside the company premises is possible.

120570.explanation: The national government has set frameworks for fireworks by groups of residents in foundations or associations. If the municipality also organizes a fireworks show, residents who do not want to form an association or foundation can meet at the local show.

120571.explanation: To truly take a big step in sustainability, we will have to buy fewer things that last longer. Products will be sustainable and repairable, which will become the norm. A repair café can contribute to longer use of items.

120573.explanation: Buildings and events must be accessible to all our residents. If entrepreneurs face financial challenges in making this possible, the municipality can think along as creatively as possible. Direct financial support to entrepreneurs is not possible for a government.

120574.explanation: If multiple generations of a family want to live on one farmstead, we should allow that as generously as possible. This also applies to informal care homes. At the same time, we are careful with our landscape environment.

120576.explanation: Independent journalism and free media play an important role in our democratic rule of law. They are watchdogs of an open society. Good local and regional journalism is also necessary for this. Money in the form of assignments is the best option.

120578.explanation: The ChristianUnion wants affordable cultural education accessible to all children. Children from families in poverty can receive discounts or participate for free. Music lessons are subsidized in any case.

120579.explanation: If it turns out that sports clubs have their affairs in order but struggle financially, the municipality supports these clubs. Sports must be accessible and affordable for all our residents. Sports clubs make an important contribution to health and resilience.

120582.explanation: We focus on close cooperation between the police and the enforcement officers (BOA’s). We equip BOA’s appropriately for the important role they play in society.

120583.explanation: Only if it is necessary for the safety of certain areas.

120587.explanation: In some cases, yes. Holiday parks that are not sustainable for the recreational sector may get a residential destination. Within Vital Holiday Parks, the frameworks are determined to make that assessment. We are also careful with our recreational sector.

120588.explanation: Housing shortage is high. This requires flexibility in housing locations. We also support flexible housing forms outside village borders. The ChristianUnion wants the municipality to facilitate residents’ housing ideas with minimal rules and as creatively as possible.

120590.explanation: People do not always manage alone, and not always even together. The municipality’s task is then to stand beside people. Residents must be able to rely on the government when their livelihood is at risk.

120591.explanation: Too many residents live below the poverty line and experience the stress of financial insecurity. The ChristianUnion Nunspeet chooses a compassionate and just poverty policy that is easy to find and simple to apply for.

120594.explanation: That is better for traffic safety, livability, and air quality.

120595.explanation: It is important that care aligns with the patient’s beliefs.

120597.explanation: We keep costs low for our residents. Property tax (OZB) will only rise with the indexation. Any motivated deviation requires a well-considered decision by the municipal council.

120599.explanation: If necessary for maintaining facilities important to tourists as well, we believe it is fair that not only local residents bear the costs, but also tourists. We are careful with our important recreational sector.

120602.explanation: Priority housing for status holders can only lapse if a realistic alternative is offered, so the asylum chain does not stall. We also have a legal task regarding housing status holders.

120604.explanation: The ChristianUnion gives opportunities to citizen initiatives and supports local energy communities, such as Nunspeet Energie. This way they truly have a role in developing large-scale energy generation via wind and solar. This can, in moderation, also take place on agricultural land.

120608.explanation: A high residential flat does not fit our village community. At the same time, we do not want to place too many barriers to providing sufficient housing for our youth. Building upward on suitable locations is acceptable.

120611.explanation: We want to first properly research the usefulness, necessity for traffic safety, and financial feasibility of a tunnel before taking further steps. If the tunnel is not needed, we prefer to spend the money on social facilities.

120558.explanation: Although CDA supports diversity, parties themselves decide who they nominate as candidates.

120562.explanation: Farmers who want to stay must have enough space to maintain a viable business. The municipality’s attitude towards expanding activities should be positive.

120563.explanation: Generating sustainable energy is a wish of the municipality; private initiatives that fit deserve support.

120566.explanation: Entrepreneurs are responsible for decent housing for workers. This can be on their own premises or other suitable locations.

120570.explanation: CDA supports organizing centralized fireworks shows. The municipality can assist, but residents/associations must organize them themselves.

120571.explanation: The municipality must ensure there are good locations to bring bulky waste. Collection by the municipality is expensive and leads to higher waste taxes; CDA does not support that.

120573.explanation: Stimulating and facilitating good accessibility is fine, but entrepreneurs are responsible for implementation.

120574.explanation: This possibility already exists in the form of self-realization (of course under conditions).

120576.explanation: A strong local broadcaster is important for objective news and information. Sufficient resources must be available to guarantee this.

120578.explanation: Children must be able to play sports and make music. Money should not be an obstacle. There already is an excellent arrangement which CDA wants to maintain.

120579.explanation: Self-reliance is the norm, but if necessary to maintain sports used by many residents, the municipality can provide limited support.

120583.explanation: Only if it is truly necessary, for example in case of persistent nightlife problems or to combat drug trafficking.

120587.explanation: Despite the housing shortage, the recreational offer must also be maintained. This is essential for a healthy economy. Tourism is an important sector in the municipality of Nunspeet.

120588.explanation: The construction of new residential areas is still necessary for now to combat the housing shortage. As far as CDA is concerned, this may be limitedly allowed outside the village boundaries as well.

120590.explanation: Self-reliance and caring for each other are the norm. But CDA believes there must also be a safety net for the vulnerable in our society.

120591.explanation: In Nunspeet, agreements have already been made for slightly more generous provisions than usual. CDA wants to maintain this, but not to expand it further.

120594.explanation: CDA considers traffic safety very important. Within built-up areas, 30 km/h should be the standard; it has been proven that this reduces the number of accidents with injuries.

120595.explanation: For many care tasks, general provision is fine, and in practice, there is often a choice of care aligned with one’s beliefs. CDA finds it important that residents can always access care providers safely and without judgment.

120597.explanation: Current policy on municipal taxes should remain. Only well-substantiated plans that serve the public interest may justify a limited increase in property tax (ozb).

120599.explanation: CDA wants to continue investing to be an attractive municipality for tourists. But tourists themselves should also contribute more financially.

120602.explanation: Every municipality must fulfill its statutory task in allocating housing to status holders. This also applies to Nunspeet. However, building homes for local residents has priority.

120604.explanation: CDA remains in favor of a pilot project, provided that local investment is a condition. For example, at a location potentially suitable for residential or business development in the future.

120608.explanation: CDA is less reserved than in the past. Building up to 4 or 5 stories in suitable locations to improve affordability is open for discussion.

120611.explanation: It is more important to realize a 'brilconstructie' (bridge-like structure) at the A28. Research must determine whether a tunnel is also necessary. CDA is cautious, especially considering the major investment required.

120558.explanation: Diversity and representation are important but cannot be captured by a strict 50/50 split between men and women; quality, inclusivity, and cooperation come first.

120562.explanation: It depends on the location. Not if it comes at the expense of nature or open meadow areas.

120563.explanation: This is the ideal picture: residents jointly produce their own green and affordable electricity with a wind turbine or solar panels. They use the electricity onsite, so the grid is not burdened. Energy cooperatives are an asset to our municipality and should be encouraged.

120566.explanation: Those who bring people to our municipality to work must ensure these workers have decent housing without burdening our residents. There is no place for exploitation and nuisance in our municipality.

120570.explanation: The best New Year's celebration is one we all enjoy together, with a fireworks show in all four centers organized or facilitated by the municipality. This keeps it safe and allows us to enjoy a pleasant New Year's Eve together.

120571.explanation: Garbage, old mattresses, and other litter on the street are annoying, unsafe, and unhealthy. The easiest way to prevent this is by collecting bulky waste for free. This avoids hassle and gives us cleaner streets.

120573.explanation: Accessibility is vital for people with disabilities. However, many important places are not yet properly accessible, for example for wheelchair users. Entrepreneurs often cannot afford such adaptations on their own. Therefore, we believe the municipality should provide support.

120574.explanation: Opposed to the hardening (paving over) of the rural area.

120576.explanation: Local media are extremely important to know what is happening in your own environment, from the street to the municipal council. But many local media struggle to stay afloat. We believe the municipality should contribute, also by purchasing more products.

120578.explanation: That is arranged through the Youth Fund for Sports and Culture (Jeugdfonds Sport- en Cultuur).

120579.explanation: Sports clubs are responsible for managing their own budgets. The municipality contributes through subsidies.

120582.explanation: The visible presence of enforcement officers (BOA’s) and more contact with our residents can have a preventive effect and contribute to our safety.

120583.explanation: Camera surveillance is repressive. Footage can be reviewed afterward. PvdA GroenLinks focuses on prevention to avoid the need for cameras.

120587.explanation: Except in the case of temporary housing units.

120588.explanation: No new construction at the expense of open meadow or nature in our rural area!

120590.explanation: If debt forgiveness of municipal taxes stops, people with the lowest incomes in our municipality will get into big trouble. They simply cannot miss that money. We believe everyone should be able to keep their head above water, which is why we support debt forgiveness.

120591.explanation: Poverty is a political choice, not ours. We support a basic income that empowers residents instead of leaving them stranded.

120594.explanation: The figures clearly show: a lower maximum speed makes traffic in our municipality much safer. For cycling children and elderly people with reduced mobility, this makes a huge difference. Moreover, the streets become quieter and the air cleaner.

120595.explanation: It is important that residents can choose either an identity-based or a non-identity-based care provider.

120597.explanation: The level of property tax (ozb) depends on the major projects to be carried out in our municipality, such as in the past the station area and our multifunctional sports park, and in coming years educational housing.

120599.explanation: Taxes should not automatically be increased.

120602.explanation: With 1,500 new homes and necessary flexible housing, we must prevent waiting lists from becoming so long that 'priority' is needed. This removes the created conflict between 'local residents' and status holders.

120604.explanation: First and foremost: PvdA GroenLinks does not want to cover our meadows with solar panels and prefers rooftops. However, we do not exclude tailor-made solutions when electricity can once again be fed back into the grid.

120611.explanation: A tunnel under the railway at Eperweg is not certainly the right solution. The problem and alternatives, such as the 'brilconstructie' and other traffic measures, must be thoroughly investigated before making major investments.

120558.explanation: Lux21 chooses quality and competence over fixed quotas. A council should be composed based on craftsmanship, experience, and vision, regardless of gender. Healthy diversity arises when the most suitable candidates are appointed, not through imposed ratios.

120562.explanation: Farmers deserve perspective and earning opportunities. Mini-campings and shops provide extra income and strengthen the sector. Lux21 wants to lower barriers for multifunctional agriculture. Relax rules within reasonable limits so farmers can innovate and make their businesses future-proof.

120563.explanation: Sustainable energy must be economically viable without municipal subsidies. Initiatives like NunspeetEnergie show cooperatives can manage themselves. Grid congestion is a national responsibility. The municipality facilitates with fast permits, information, and advice, not with funding. This promotes sustainability.

120566.explanation: Employers who need seasonal workers are responsible for their housing. We simplify placing studios or chalets on company premises so employers can easily realize this and prevent problems with living conditions.

120570.explanation: A spectacular municipal fireworks show is a positive response to the fireworks debate. Lux21 wants to organize this together with local entrepreneurs and link it to hospitality activities. This keeps New Year's celebration festive and safe, respecting our tradition of celebrating together.

120571.explanation: Residents should be able to dispose of their bulky waste for free. We opt for an accessible recycling center where residents can deliver 300-500 kg per year free of charge. This is more efficient and cheaper than home collection. Residents already pay waste levies - this fits well with that.

120573.explanation: Entrepreneurs are themselves responsible for the accessibility of their premises. By improving the business climate, they gain more financial scope for investments. An organization like Stichting Accessibility can provide expertise and advice; we must ensure a healthy local economy.

120574.explanation: Farmers and landowners should be able to use their land more smartly for homes, informal care, or small-scale businesses. By relaxing zoning plans, we provide room for initiative and accelerate housing construction. The housing shortage calls for smart solutions.

120576.explanation: Local broadcasters are important for democracy and are under pressure. Lux21 agrees with support. In this council term, not through extra funding, but via collaboration such as joint media productions and municipal communication. From 2028, the national government will take over part of the financing.

120578.explanation: We mainly want to create a healthy climate for associations and schools so that music education is accessible. For children, there is the existing Sports and Culture scheme (€400 per year) which we maintain. This allows children to take music lessons.

120579.explanation: Sports clubs are the heart of our community. We support unforeseen situations of general interest, but expect personal responsibility. When providing financial aid, we link temporary guidance to get the club healthy again. No structural support in case of mismanagement.

120582.explanation: BOAs fill gaps left by the police due to limited capacity. We want more BOAs for safety and visible supervision, such as at the station. Parking management becomes a separate service with random checks. BOAs are not for minor annoyances, but for real safety.

120583.explanation: Privacy is valuable. Cameras create an impression of control and have limited effect on nuisance. We focus first on neighborhood prevention and visible enforcement officers. Only at specific hotspots with demonstrable safety risks do we consider cameras, always temporary and well explained.

120587.explanation: We prioritize housing over recreation. Holiday homes that are regularly empty can be converted to permanent residence with clear policy frameworks, such as clustering permanent residents on the park. This immediately provides shelter for starting home seekers and gives parks year-round income.

120588.explanation: The housing shortage is too high to exclude building locations. With thoughtful policy, building outside the built-up area is possible, provided the village character is preserved. We build first on mapped locations but do not rule out further expansion if necessary for affordable housing.

120590.explanation: Lux21 wants to end automatic waiver of municipal taxes. Instead, we focus on budget counseling and debt assistance so people gain control over their finances. Support remains possible through customized solutions, but not as a standard arrangement.

120591.explanation: We maintain the current income limit (social assistance norm), which is designed and indexed for such matters. Instead of raising the limit, we help with budget counseling and debt assistance, also preventively for people just above the threshold. This promotes self-reliance and prevents poverty.

120594.explanation: On main roads such as avenues and industrial roads, 50 km/h is a fine speed. With recently adjusted priority situations, traffic situations are safe. Generally, 30 km/h impedes flow and is unenforceable. We choose tailored solutions: 30 km/h where really needed, 50 km/h where possible.

120595.explanation: People should feel comfortable with their care provider. Freedom of choice is important but cannot lead to extra municipal costs. The municipality ensures good, accessible basic care. Specific preferences based on religion or culture are a personal responsibility.

120597.explanation: Property tax must go down. House prices rise sharply due to the housing shortage-we do not punish residents for that. Homeownership must be encouraged, not restrained with high taxes. Lower property tax gives people more financial room and makes living more affordable.

120599.explanation: Tourists place high demands on our infrastructure, facilities, and nature. There may be something in return. With over a million overnight stays per year, a fair contribution is justified. The extra money is visibly invested in amenities, nature management, and events.

120602.explanation: Social housing is primarily for Nunspeters. Home seekers wait for years. That is why we legally strive to limit the share of status holders as much as possible and seek alternative housing such as temporary solutions, without further increasing pressure on the regular housing market.

120604.explanation: We are against bans-they rarely solve problems. Farmers deserve perspective. If solar panels on farmland contribute to their income model, it should be possible within reasonable frameworks. Multifunctional agriculture strengthens the sector. Preference for roofs due to landscape impact.

120608.explanation: At suitable locations like Nieuw Feithenhof, residential flats of a maximum of six to seven floors are possible. A gradual building height transition works better than strong contrasts. No high-rise that harms village appearance, farmland, or monuments. We value aesthetics and want to avoid monotonous high-rise.

120611.explanation: This is a logical goal for safety and traffic flow, provided the connection to the A28 is also addressed with an overpass structure. This requires financing and cooperation with the province and national government. We proactively examine feasibility within our financial possibilities as a municipality.

120558.explanation: VVD Nunspeet looks at quality and suitability. A good representation is nice, but the best people should be chosen based on content and experience.

120562.explanation: VVD Nunspeet supports room for farmers to expand, but not “always.” It must fit the environment, traffic, nature, and rules, with fast and predictable permits.

120563.explanation: VVD Nunspeet supports local energy cooperatives and citizen initiatives, such as joint production and storage, so that proceeds and support remain local.

120566.explanation: Employers must take responsibility for good, safe housing. The municipality must strictly enforce against abuses and nuisance.

120570.explanation: A fireworks show can be enjoyable but is not a core task of the municipality. VVD Nunspeet opts for financial responsibility and prioritizing housing, safety, and facilities. Preferably organized through initiatives by entrepreneurs or associations.

120571.explanation: VVD Nunspeet wants to prevent dumping and keep waste collection practical. Free bulky waste pickup is not desirable unlimitedly. Allowing residents to deliver a certain amount of bulky waste free of charge at a depot a few times a year is also a good option.

120573.explanation: Accessibility is important. The municipality can stimulate and think along, but financial support must be focused on impact and feasibility, not as a standard scheme.

120574.explanation: VVD Nunspeet wants to make “living on one’s own plot” possible, but not unlimited. It must fit spatial quality, accessibility, and the rural area.

120576.explanation: Independent journalism is essential for oversight and trust. VVD Nunspeet wants a media fund to stimulate local journalism. Moreover, the local broadcaster plays an important role in community life and making local initiatives visible.

120578.explanation: Culture must be accessible, but structural subsidies must be fair and purposeful. VVD Nunspeet supports opportunities for children, preferably through smart arrangements and cooperation.

120579.explanation: Associations are the backbone of the community. The municipality may temporarily assist if needed, but support must be fair, transparent, and not create structural dependence.

120582.explanation: More enforcement against nuisance, illegal dumping, and traffic safety is needed. BOAs enhance livability, especially regarding dumping and noise problems.

120583.explanation: Camera surveillance can help at hotspots, but not everywhere. VVD wants targeted use where it demonstrably contributes to safety.

120587.explanation: VVD Nunspeet wants to enable temporary residence in holiday homes with clear conditions. This offers short-term perspective for home seekers. From this temporary solution, one can move on to regular housing without having to queue again at the end of the waiting list.

120588.explanation: The housing shortage requires building both within and, where necessary, at the edges. VVD Nunspeet wants to accelerate in all centers, focusing on affordability and flow, without unnecessarily damaging the landscape.

120590.explanation: VVD Nunspeet wants people to participate. Waivers and targeted support may be necessary, preferably temporary and with perspective, so residents do not get into further trouble.

120591.explanation: VVD Nunspeet wants no one left out. Help must also be accessible for people just above the threshold, through temporary support like appliance schemes or energy funds.

120594.explanation: 30 km/h is not logical everywhere. VVD Nunspeet wants targeted measures where it is unsafe and extra enforcement on speed, instead of a generic measure.

120595.explanation: Care should suit the person. Freedom of choice and customization are important, as long as quality and accessibility remain guaranteed.

120597.explanation: If there is financial room, costs must go down. VVD Nunspeet supports frugal and effective policy and money left over returned to residents and entrepreneurs.

120599.explanation: Tourists are important for Nunspeet and generate spending at businesses. Therefore, VVD Nunspeet does not want to raise the tourist tax. We should mainly ensure that revenues are effectively spent and do not permanently disappear into organizations without clear added value.

120602.explanation: VVD Nunspeet does not want priority for status holders: first our own residents and home seekers with local ties. At the same time, we remain honest about the law: housing status holders is a legal task, so in practice they often still receive urgency/priority.

120604.explanation: VVD Nunspeet wants sustainable energy, but with prudence. First on roofs and smart solutions such as storage and cooperatives. Solar fields are only possible if done carefully and appropriately.

120608.explanation: VVD Nunspeet wants to build what is needed, but fitting the village scale and surroundings. High-rise buildings can be in logical locations, but not as a standard solution everywhere.

120611.explanation: Accessibility and safety are priorities. VVD Nunspeet wants to improve the A28/Eperweg project and preferably realize a simultaneous tunnel under the railway.

statements.title: Nunspeet should allocate funds for a tunnel under the railway on the Eperweg.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: Nunspeet should hire more municipal enforcement officers (boa's) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
There should be more camera surveillance in Nunspeet.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: Tall residential apartment blocks should be allowed in Nunspeet.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
